codeReview.summary: The link-autocomplete feature implementation is solid with proper TypeScript types, Vue 3 composition API, and Tiptap extension patterns. All 19 unit tests pass. The feature correctly fulfills VAL-BACKLINKS-002 by showing page suggestions when typing [[.

issues.description: WIKI_LINK_REGEX uses global 'g' flag which maintains state across matches. For InputRule usage, the global flag is unnecessary and could cause unexpected behavior in edge cases.
issues.suggestedFix: Remove the 'g' flag: const WIKI_LINK_REGEX = /\[\[([^\]]+)\]\]/g; → const WIKI_LINK_REGEX = /\[\[([^\]]+)\]\]/;

issues.description: The fallback code uses 'state.schema.text(' ') as any' which has incorrect typing. The text() method creates a TextNode/Fragment, not something that should be cast with 'as any' for insertion.
issues.suggestedFix: Use proper transaction method: tr.insert(from, state.schema.text(' '));

issues.description: Popup positioning uses fixed offset (rect.bottom + 8) without viewport boundary checks. The popup could render off-screen for content near the bottom of the viewport.
issues.suggestedFix: Add viewport boundary detection: Check if popup fits within window.innerHeight and adjust position if needed.

issues.description: The items filtering function has no result limit. With large wikis (1000+ pages), all matching results are returned which could impact performance.
issues.suggestedFix: Consider adding a limit: return filtered.slice(0, 10); for optimal UX.

issues.description: The computed property 'filteredItems' doesn't actually filter - it just returns props.items. The filtering is expected to happen in the parent component. The naming could be misleading.
issues.suggestedFix: Rename to 'suggestionItems' or add a comment explaining this is intentionally passthrough.

summary: The link-autocomplete feature successfully implements VAL-BACKLINKS-002. Typing [[ triggers autocomplete suggestions with existing pages and a 'Create new page' option. All 19 tests pass. Minor issues found include a regex global flag concern, incorrect typing in fallback code, and suggestions for viewport boundary handling and result limiting, but none are blocking.
